artifacts.title: Good receipt from 2026-02-23
artifacts.excerpt:
cool eng problem from mining conf:

you want to blast mine, ie: drill a grid of holes, fill them with explosives, and blow it up, then collect the fragments for processing.

but you’re quite close to a residential area. how do you prevent the soundwaves from getting to them?

answer: you can stagger the detonations by ~15ms, so the sound waves destructively interfere with each other, reducing perceived volume by ~70%
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: good, work, don, better.

artifacts.title: Really receipt from 2026-02-28
artifacts.excerpt:
it’s really weird hearing about this senior archetype that I’ve only ever seen in relatively junior engineers 

the senior-most by title and authority engineers I’ve worked with have always been super pliable, pragmatic, w ego attached to broad victory over specific technical decisions.

perhaps this downstream of working in rather functional organizations?
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: really, often, don, want.

artifacts.title: Really receipt from 2025-10-25
artifacts.excerpt:
Very often, I spend an afternoon with a friend that I’ve not seen in many years, and I’m incredibly surprised by this sense that personality growth is real and even the default.

Like there’s a marked change that happens quite often in their 20s, where people seem to step-change to become more mellow, personable, happier, calmer.

Kinda more well-rounded, more in their element. A lot of their sharpest edges & most grating traits sanded down. Many of the more neurotic ones seemingly lost a lot of neuroticism along the way.

On reflection, this seems to be true for >80% of the friends I’ve known for over 5 years.

This really didn’t have to be the median personality gradient, and I’m quite heartened & grateful that it is.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: really, often, don, want.

planets.name: Kinda
planets.summary: Archive cluster around kinda: 140 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Kinda receipt from 2025-10-20
artifacts.excerpt:
kinda brutal how much of a gambling ad this looks like.

the primary Kalshi/Polymarket bull thesis unfortunately looks like: 

- the real drive behind sports betting was the betting, and the sports was merely the Lindy container.

- providing opportunities to bet on more cerebral events will successfully draw in a newer & wealthier demographic.

sad possibility that the ultimate legacy of prediction markets might be as a mechanism to launder gambling and bypass traditional antibodies against it

a white pill hypothesis is that this helps people develop better epistemics through this strong, painful signal. and that inculcation of truth-seeking is worth the costs of gambling. but I’m not sure if that’s true?
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: kinda, seems, too, hard.

planets.name: Too
planets.summary: Archive cluster around too: 144 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Too receipt from 2026-05-04
artifacts.excerpt:
The inelasticity of Waymo supply is so fun!

Presumably, if Lyft supply outpaces demand too much, drivers get no rides and call it a night. And if demand outpaces supply, drivers make anomalously high $/h thus drive longer shifts, or get a notif to start driving.

But Waymo has neither knob of rapidly adjusting supply, thus can only entice or dampen demand.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: too, probably, use, most.

artifacts.title: Too receipt from 2026-01-22
artifacts.excerpt:
I have occasionally worked with weak engineers that often unknowingly wrote code that would have severe side effects + were very put off if you pointed out significant issues in PR review. (Vibes of ~why are you being the bad guy and not letting me ship and just be done)

Fortunately: this was usually self limiting, these traits usually correlated with far fewer PRs per week too.

I’ve not been in a big SWE team since Claude Code, but I wonder to what extent the 25th percentile methodicalness SWE in a large team is much more of a liability now.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: too, probably, use, most.
